questions: what happens when internet goes down?
answers: The internet is a global network of interconnected computers that communicate using standardized protocols like TCP/IP. Data travels in small packets through routers and servers to reach its destination.
Internet holo ekta bishal network jekhane millions of computers connected thake. Data packets akare information travel kore routers ar servers er modhye diye.

WiFi is a wireless networking technology that allows devices to connect to the internet without physical cables, using radio waves. Your router converts the wired internet signal into a wireless one that your devices can pick up.
An ISP (Internet Service Provider) is a company that provides you access to the internet. Examples include Grameenphone, Banglalink, and local broadband providers in Bangladesh.
Bandwidth is the maximum amount of data that can be transferred over an internet connection in a given time, usually measured in Mbps. Higher bandwidth means faster downloads and smoother streaming.
DNS (Domain Name System) works like a phonebook for the internet. When you type a website name like google.com, DNS translates it into an IP address so your browser knows where to find the server.

If your internet is slow, try restarting your router, checking for too many connected devices, moving closer to the WiFi router, or contacting your ISP. Sometimes background downloads or updates also slow things down.

TCP/IP is the foundational protocol suite of the internet. TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) ensures reliable data delivery, while IP (Internet Protocol) handles addressing and routing packets to the correct destination.
